Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.summary:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.

Continue reading...

Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters.

The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters.

The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife, Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”.

Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close

Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby.

Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica.

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.summary:
In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat

Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common.

The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations.

The group of more than 100 people have been sent indefinitely to the tiny Pacific island while they wait for their asylum claims to be processed. Many of the cohort say they are skipping meals to make ends meet, according to a new survey by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre (ASRC) advocacy group.

More than a decade after the human microbiome was comprehensively mapped, scientists have now identified the vast communities of microbes that make up the Great Barrier Reef.

Analysing DNA from seawater samples of 48 reefs, researchers have mapped the microbiome of the Great Barrier Reef, documenting more than 360,000 distinct viruses as well as more than 500 new bacterial species not previously known to science.

Europe’s leaders queued up this week to welcome Andy Burnham as Britain’s seventh prime minister since the Brexit vote – all expressing the hope that his tenure would continue the thaw in EU-UK relations initiated by his predecessor, Keir Starmer.

Ursula von der Leyen, the European Commission chief, said she was looking forward to strengthening cross-Channel ties, adding that a “very good” first discussion had confirmed a common commitment to “bringing the EU and UK closer and closer”. France’s Emmanuel Macron said he hoped for a deeper relationship that gave “new momentum to the partnership” between the EU and the UK. Germany’s Friedrich Merz said he, too, was looking forward to forging an even closer bond.

Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year.

“Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to social media.

events.title: Wednesday briefing: How Trump’s Iran gamble has produced a crisis reshaping the global order
events.summary:
In today’s newsletter: ​A White House operating without expertise, strategy or restraint ​is stuck in a conflict with no easy way out, which is sending economic shockwaves far beyond its own borders

Good morning. Within two hours of entering No 10 on Monday afternoon, Andy Burnham had his first call with Donald Trump. After chit-chatting about the football (both agreed England had an “excellent tournament”) and Manchester (our new prime minister invited the president to visit the city Trump once described as a “town”), they got on to far trickier territory: the strait of Hormuz.

The US has attacked Iran for the eleventh consecutive night, hitting aircraft hangars and drone storage sites across the country. It came after Iran launched retaliatory strikes on US air bases and neighbouring Gulf states, which have accused it of deliberately targeting desalination plants that are essential to life in the region. Each side now claims it is preventing the other from gaining control over the strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil was shipped before the war began. Vessels carrying crucial shipments of oil, gas and fertiliser remain at a standstill.

UK politics | Andy Burnham is under mounting pressure to explain how he will pay for his policy pledges as he implored his new cabinet to examine “all possible ways” to tackle the cost of living crisis.

UK news | A man has appeared in court charged with the murder of the former MP and Reform UK spokesperson Ann Widdecombe. Joshua Kerry, 28, is accused of killing the former Conservative minister at her home in Haytor, Devon, by hitting her on the head with a hammer 21 times.

Middle East | The US defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, told the Senate the US-Israel war on Iran had cost $37.5bn so far, as he faced lawmakers furious over the conflict’s soaring price tag and the deaths last week of three American service members.

Climate crisis | About 23 million people will be banned from using hosepipes by this weekend, as England and Wales moves closer to drought status.

Ukraine | Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests.

Continue reading...

events.title: Iran’s foreign minister describes moment of attack that killed Khamenei
events.summary:
Abbas Araghchi says he has kept on the move since being pulled from rubble after strike on first day of war

Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has described how he was pulled from the rubble of a leadership compound after the US-Israeli airstrike that killed the country’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war, and has survived since by constantly moving around the capital, Tehran, by car.

In an interview with the Iranian journalist Javad Mogouyi, Araghchi confirmed Israel and the US had accurate intelligence on the location of Khamenei and other officials, enabling them to target Iran’s senior leadership.

events.summary:
Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals

India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement.

Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan to quit.

events.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
events.summary:
Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday

Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.”

On Tuesday, central Delhi still thronged with thousands of protesters, as an unprecedented expression of dissent against the powerful ruling Narendra Modi government showed no sign of abating.

events.title: Rescuers in Afghanistan search for missing and dead after flash floods destroy homes
events.summary:
National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in eastern province of Nuristan

Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of houses destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities said had killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing.

The Afghanistan national disaster management authority said on Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 had been injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the eastern province of Nuristan on Monday, causing an undetermined number of buildings to collapse. The provincial capital city of Parun was among the areas affected, authorities said.

Tens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.

A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight.

events.title: ‘When I get home, my whole body aches’: extreme heat saps health and wages of India’s poorest workers
events.summary:
Exclusive: Outdoor workers losing up to 9% of income as weather becomes ‘poverty multiplier’, research says

Extreme heat is pushing some of India’s poorest workers further into poverty, with income losses of up to 9% for outdoor workers, new research has found.

Analysis done by the International Institute for Environment and Development (IIED), and shared exclusively with the Guardian, found that extreme heat is acting as a “poverty multiplier” for hundreds of millions of workers in India’s informal sector.

events.title: Ex-Southern Water boss to face trial over allegedly manipulating sewage testing
events.summary:
Former chief executive Matthew Wright and three further ex-employees named in criminal proceedings by Environment Agency

The former chief executive of Southern Water is being prosecuted for conspiracy to defraud along with three other former employees, over allegations they manipulated sewage wastewater testing results to avoid tens of millions of pounds in fines.

Matthew Wright is the first water industry boss to face a criminal charge over sewage discharges. He and three others are charged with conspiracy to defraud the Environment Agency and ​the water regulator, Ofwat, between 2012 and 2017 by ⁠creating artificial “no-flow” ​events ​at wastewater treatment ​works to ​influence ‌compliance checks.

The prime minister plans to make Sarwar, who was the first senior Labour figure to call for Starmer to resign, a peer and give him a ministerial post at the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade.

Approximately a billion tiny plastic nurdles have entered the River Tyne after a “devastating spill”, with the pellets thought to have spread much further than first thought.

Nurdles, which are usually 1mm-5mm in size, act as the raw material to manufacture most plastic products.

Wes Streeting said the US president was “right” to demand Europe pays more for its own defence.

Donald Trump has been increasingly critical of Europe for relying too much on the US for its security, and has demanded Nato allies reach 5% of GDP on defence spending by 2035.

President Trump has called on Europe to carry a greater share of the burden for our own defence, and he is right to do so. The UK has always been a leading light on defence spending, but the world is changing and in these dangerous uncertain times it is clear that we have needed to go further.

That’s why we’ve committed £298bn over the next four years, including an additional £15bn on top of last year’s spending review settlement. At the recent Nato summit, all members reaffirmed our commitment to 3.5 by 35 [3.5% of GDP by 2035].

The ousted Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, will go on trial on US drug trafficking charges starting on 1 June ⁠2027, a judge said ⁠on Wednesday.

Company behind ChatGPT says agent ‘cheated’ an evaluation by attacking a Hugging Face database

OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, accessed the open web and hacked a prominent startup by itself in an “unprecedented incident”.

Congressional Progressive caucus say ‘healthcare should not be a privilege’ as they champions single payer system

The Congressional Progressive caucus (CPC) held a shadow hearing on Medicare for All on Wednesday, led by Pramila Jayapal and Debbie Dingell.

The hearing comes as over half of the House Democratic caucus has co-sponsored Medicare for All legislation, and Senator Andy Kim introduced legislation on Tuesday that would enroll all children into Medicare until the age of 26, while Republicans passed drastic healthcare cuts in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including nearly $1n in cuts to Medicaid and allowing Affordable Care Act subsidies to expire.

events.summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

events.title: Why Indonesia's economy is under pressure
events.summary: Southeast Asia's largest economy risks losing its emerging-market status as President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans rattle markets. Can he deliver growth?

events.title: Why is Zelenskyy reshuffling Ukraine's government?
events.summary: Ukraine's Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has resigned, prompting a Cabinet reshuffle led by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. What comes next?

events.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
events.summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

events.title: How the 2016 coup attempt changed Turkey for good
events.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

events.title: Germany's Merz receives death threat from Iranian newspaper
events.summary: In retaliation for Khamenei's death, a Tehran daily listed 13 world leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, as targets. Politicians voiced concern.

events.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
events.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

events.title: Ukraine parliament accepts PM Svyrydenko's resignation
events.summary: With Ukraine "changing its political strategy," President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is trying to remake the government,

events.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
events.summary: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

events.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
events.summary: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

events.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
events.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

events.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
events.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

events.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
events.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

events.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
events.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

events.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
events.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

events.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
events.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

events.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
events.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

events.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
events.summary: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

events.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
events.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

events.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
events.summary: Supermarket billionaire Dieter Schwarz is investing billions in his cloud company, Schwarz Digits, to rival Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

events.title: Ukraine's Bidnyi 'outraged' by Russia's Olympic return
events.summary: In exclusive comments to DW, Ukraine's sports minister, Matviy Bidnyi, said he is outraged by the IOC opening the door to Russia's return to the Olympics

events.title: When London stank to high heaven
events.summary: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

events.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

events.title: Why the World Cup can be good for your mental health
events.summary: The World Cup can offer more than just entertainment, it can also benefit your mental well-being. DW explains why.

events.title: What's behind Germany's World Cup woes?
events.summary: Germany have endured three straight disappointing World Cups. Experts say the reasons why are connected to football, but also to German society.

events.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
events.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

events.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
events.summary: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

events.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
events.summary: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

events.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
events.summary: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

events.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
events.summary: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

events.title: The Australian tree supercharging global wildfires
events.summary: As Europe experiences another summer of extreme heat and wildfire warnings, one tree imported from Australia is coming under renewed scrutiny across the world: eucalyptus.

events.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
events.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

events.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
events.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

events.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
events.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

events.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
events.summary: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

events.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
events.summary: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

events.title: Why Volkswagen's huge workforce became a costly burden
events.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

events.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
events.summary: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
events.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

events.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
events.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

events.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
events.summary: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

events.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
events.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

events.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
events.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

events.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
events.summary: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
events.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

events.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
events.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

events.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
events.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

events.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
events.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
events.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

events.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
events.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

events.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
events.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

events.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
events.summary: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

events.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
events.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: Experts axed by Trump keep climate information flowing
events.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead
events.summary: Daniel Siad, ⁠a modelling scout with ​ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been ​found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday. Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

events.title: Exclusive: In Kobane, Syria's Kurdish-majority city, postwar integration processes get underway
events.summary: FRANCE 24’s Wassim Nasr traveled to Kobane, the northern Syrian city that became the symbol of Kurdish resistance during the civil war, to report on recent efforts to integrate Kurdish and Syrian government forces.

events.title: Drug gang threats force French police to put southern mayor under protection
events.summary: The mayor of Alès in southern France was taken from his home at night by an elite police unit amid fears of an imminent attack by the country’s most dreaded drug gang, officials said Tuesday. Christophe Rivenq had previously received death threats linked to the DZ Mafia gang, which has grown out of its Marseille base to become one of France’s main security threats.

events.title: 'Cockroach Janta Party' takes India by storm
events.summary:
Born from a satirical retort to a passing comment by India's top judge, the "Cockroach Janta Party" has grown into a full-fledged movement protesting the government's mishandling of the nationwide medical entrance examination in May.
 
Despite a violent crackdown on a peaceful march heading to the Indian parliament in New Delhi on Monday, members of the movement have kept up their protest.
 
FRANCE 24 explains how the CJP came to be.

events.title: France's D-Day landing beaches could become a UNESCO World Heritage site in July
events.summary: Normandy’s application to have the D-Day beaches in northern France listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site could finally be accepted at the end of July, after it's submission nearly 20 years ago. The nomination also includes Pointe du Hoc, the German artillery battery at Longues-sur-Mer, Port Mulberry, and numerous military cemeteries and memorials. The UN cultural agency is set to male a decision between July 24 and 27.

events.title: #Gaza: #Mamdani urges government to arrest #Netanyahu if he visits #US
events.summary: In a video posted to social media on July 21, #NYC mayor Zohran #Mamdani urges the US federal government to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin #Netanyahu if he visits the #US later this year, after admitting he does not have the authority to enforce the #ICC's arrest warrant.

events.title: Philipsen wins Tour de France 17th stage as Pogacar retains overall lead
events.summary: Belgium's Jasper Philipsen won the 17th stage of the 2026 Tour de France on Wednesday with a comfortable lead ahead of Mauro Schmid and Olav Kooij at the end of the Alpine stage. While Tadej Pogacar struggled throughout the stage after suffering from a stomach problem the previous night, the Slovenian cyclist remains in the lead.

events.title: Who is Mikhailo Drapaty, Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
events.summary: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. The former head of the Ukrainian armed forces Oleksandr Syrsky has been replaced by the popular Mykhailo Drapaty. Who is he?

events.title: 'Unprecedented': France records 5,764 excess deaths during June-July heatwave period
events.summary: France recorded 5,764 more deaths than the normal rate between June 17 and July 2, health authorities said Wednesday. While authorities said the excess mortality rate was due to "all causes", the period coincided with a stretch of extreme heatwaves in several French regions.

events.title: France: Macron refuses environment minister's resignation over pesticide law
events.summary: France's environment minister will remain in her post despite her disagreement with a bill reintroducing controversial pesticides, given the "urgency" to act on climate change, her team said Wednesday. Monique Barbut had submitted her resignation to President Emmanuel Macron, but he refused it. Explanation by FRANCE 24 French politics editor Marc Perelman.

events.title: US to announce landmark deal allowing Saudis to enrich nuclear fuel, reports say
events.summary: The US on Wednesday is set to announce a deal with Saudi Arabia that would allow the oil-rich Gulf nation to enrich uranium, according to US media reports. Riyadh has long asserted its right to a civilian nuclear programme, but Israel and several policy experts warn that it could spark a nuclear proliferation threat in the region.

events.title: Senegal's Bassirou Diomaye Faye backs Macky Sall for UN top job
events.summary: Senegal will support Macky Sall’s candidacy for the post of Secretary-General of the United Nations. The announcement was made in a press release on Monday, three days after he was granted an audience with current President Bassirou Diomaye Faye. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Dakar, Aminatou Diallo.

events.title: Two foreign jihadists arrested in Nigeria for helping local terror groups
events.summary: After 17 years, the Nigeria military’s efforts to contend a jihadist insurgency do not seem to be successful. The military is facing fresh hurdles with foreign fighters, including those from the Middle East joining the insurgents. The foreigners are bringing skills and technology that have proved very valuable in the Jihadists’ fight against the military. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Lagos, Sam Olukoya.

events.title: Mounting violence in Mali poses threat to military-led government
events.summary: Mali’s military has suffered one of its deadliest losses in years. At least 50 soldiers were killed in an ambush near the northern town of Anefis, in an attack claimed by Tuareg separatists and Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists. The assault is the latest in a string of setbacks, with militants also targeting Malian and Russian Africa Corps forces, downing a military helicopter, and briefly seizing territory. More with Hénio Nsaibia, West Africa Senior Analyst at ACLED.

events.title: India: Youth-led 'Cockroach' protests increase pressure on Modi's government
events.summary: With each passing day, India's youth-led cockroach movement seems to swell. As the sit-in by anti-corruption campaigners grows in New Delhi, opposition lawmakers relay their call for the education minister to resign over the leaking of exam tests. The standoff grew with the Supreme Court refusing to hear a police brutality petition launched after Monday's crackdown on those trying to reach parliament. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in New Delhi, Sarah Jacob.

events.title: Trump to announce US-Saudi nuclear deal to allow fuel enrichment
events.summary: The New York Times is reporting that the Trump administration plans to announce that it has reached a broad nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia which could lead to the country enriching its own fuel for nuclear reactors.What do we know about the deal and what will this mean for the region? Details by Angela Diffley, FRANCE 24 international affairs editor.

events.title: Indian students vow to continue 'cockroach movement' protests despite crackdown
events.summary: Protests continue for a third day in the Indian capital, New Delhi, as pressure mounts on Narendra Modi to sack his education minister. The youth-led movement remains defiant even after Monday's heavy handed police crackdown. While the minister in question says the government remains committed to reform, the so-called Cockroach party say he's incompetent, and they want him out. Details by Delano D'Souza.

events.title: The Iran war has cost the US $37.5 billion dollars, says Defense Secretary Pete Hesgseth
events.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a Senate committee that the Pentagon needs nearly 70 billion dollars in additional funding for the war in Iran. The hearing turned combative as Senators demanded explanations as to the military's strategy to win or otherwise end the war. Story by Bryan Quinn.

events.title: Louvre heist: 'We have recovered millions worth of artworks thanks to tipsters', explains
events.summary: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Christopher Marinello, veteran lawyer, founder of Art Recovery International and one of the world's foremost experts in recovering stolen, looted, and missing works of art. The reopening of the Apollo Gallery at the Louvre, nine months after one of the most brazen museum thefts in recent history, marks more than the restoration of a famous exhibition space. It exposes a broader tension between public access to cultural heritage and the growing vulnerability of museums in an era of increasingly sophisticated organised crime.

events.title: Germany greenlights controversial joint French-Russian nuclear project
events.summary: Germany's environment ministry on Wednesday approved a French-Russian project to produce fuel rods used in Russian-designed reactors in the northwestern German town of Lingen. The joint venture project by a subsidiary of French company Framatome and Russia's state-owned Rosatom sparked fears of Russian spying, but German authorities said they had "no legal possibility to reject" it.

events.title: #Ukraine's controversial top general replaced amid mass #protests
events.summary: #Ukraine's top general Oleksander #Syrsky is being replaced by general Mikhailo #Dapraty after days of #protests demanding his departure. Syrsky was accused of having forced the popular #defence minister Mikhailo #Fedorov out as he was reticent to engage with reforms to modernise the #army.

events.title: D. Rykner says the Louvre needs to be 'refurbished', Macron's big project is 'useless and expensive'
events.summary: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Didier Rykner, Art Historian, Investigative Journalist and Founder / Editor in Chief of La Tribune de l’Art. Offering a forceful critique of the Louvre's priorities, Rykner argues that the museum's challenge is not a lack of resources, rather a misallocation of them. While acknowledging that security is a both necessary and inevitable expense, he contends that the greater threat to the Louvre lies in the deterioration of its historic fabric and the diversion of funding towards what he regards as an unnecessary, politically driven architectural expansion.

events.title: Pete Hegseth asks US Senate for 'urgent' Iran war funding increase
events.summary: The cost of the US military campaign against Iran has already cost 37.5 billion dollars. The figure was revealed by the US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth as he appeared before the Senate to make the case for the war and ask for an additional 67 billion dollars worth of funding. The move drew criticism from both Democrats and Republicans. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi.

events.title: US trial date set for Venezuela's ousted president Maduro
events.summary: Venezuela's captured president Nicolas Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores appeared in a US court today over drugs trafficking charges.

events.title: Zelenskyy left with little choice but to sack his army chief - and it's a spectacular fall from grace
events.summary: In a significant move in the middle of a war, Ukraine's president has sacked his military chief and is seeking to woo a former defence minister back into the fold – just days after ousting him too.

events.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead at Paris home
events.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris.

events.title: Rebel Wilson cleared of defaming star of her film on Instagram
events.summary: Rebel Wilson has been cleared of defaming another actress on social media.

events.title: France approves blanket social media ban for under-15s
events.summary: France has approved a blanket ban on under-15 children using social media, the first European country to introduce such a restriction.

events.title: Wreck of Pan Am plane found 74 years after ocean crash
events.summary: The wreckage of a Pan Am plane that crashed 74 years ago, and led to safety briefings becoming mandatory on flights, has been found off the coast of Puerto Rico.

events.title: Hitler's birthplace reopens as police station to deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: The building Adolf Hitler was born in has been converted into a police station, in a bid by the Austrian government to prevent it becoming a pilgrimage site for neo-Nazis.

events.title: Mass mid-air brawl forces easyJet plane to return to Tenerife
events.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced a Liverpool-bound easyJet plane to return to Tenerife.

events.title: New defence secretary hits out at Russia after live-fire exercise 40 miles off UK coast
events.summary: New defence secretary Wes Streeting has criticised a live-fire military exercise carried out by a Russian warship around 40 nautical miles (46 miles) off the British coast, calling it "performative and irresponsible".

events.title: At least 10 dead in India tunnel disaster with 17 feared trapped
events.summary: At least 10 people have died in a disaster inside an under-construction tunnel in India.

events.title: France recorded 5,700 more deaths than usual during its historic June heatwave
events.summary: France experienced at least 5,700 more deaths than usual during a historic heatwave last month, according to the public health agency

events.title: Vatican defends secrecy in sensitive abuse case involving ex-Jesuit artist Marko Rupnik
events.summary: The Vatican has confirmed that a church trial against a famous ex-Jesuit priest accused of spiritual and sexual abuse of women is ongoing

events.title: Firefighters in central Spain battle huge wildfire during heat wave
events.summary: Firefighters in central Spain are battling a wildfire that has burned over 32,000 hectares in one of the largest blazes in recent memory for the southern European country

events.title: Hitler’s birthplace in Austria reopens as a police station to deter Nazi pilgrimages
events.summary: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Austria has been inaugurated as a police station

events.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak has killed nearly 1,000 people
events.summary: At least 999 people have died from the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, according to official data published overnight into Wednesday, a grim toll in what’s already been declared the fastest Ebola outbreak in history

events.title: A protest movement is challenging India's establishment. What to know about the 'Cockroaches'
events.summary: A joke has turned into a major protest movement in India, challenging Prime Minister Narendra Modi Hindu nationalist’s government

events.title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after a brazen heist, but no jewels on display
events.summary: The Louvre's Apollo gallery is reopening to the public for the first time since last year's crown jewels heist

events.title: Iran launches missiles at Jordan
events.summary: Missile alert sirens have sounded in Jordan’s port city of Aqaba as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that has collapsed

events.title: Lebanon's prime minister calls for Israeli military withdrawal
events.summary: Lebanon's prime minister has visited a southern village as a Lebanese-Israeli agreement brokered by Washington appears to have started following a monthslong war

events.title: Toxic gas slows efforts to rescue 13 workers trapped in India hydropower tunnel
events.summary: Authorities say 13 workers are trapped inside a collapsed hydropower tunnel in India

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.summary: Ukraine is continuing its fight against Russia with new military leadership

events.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive prehistoric skeleton
events.summary: More than 2,000 volunteers are helping Polish scientists restore a Mastodonsaurus skeleton

events.title: Record fish kill reported in Sint Maarten as crews struggle to remove carcasses
events.summary: Thousands of dead fish have blanketed a canal in Sint Maarten

events.title: Two of Puerto Rico's top government officials to step down as corruption allegations persist
events.summary: Puerto Rico’s governor says that her embattled chief of staff, Francisco Domenech, and deputy chief of staff, Itza García, are stepping down next month as they deny corruption allegations

events.summary: Firefighters are battling massive wildfires across Europe, stemming from an ongoing heatwave that's now hitting Sicily. NBC News' Claudio Lavanga reports on the fire burning out of control there, as the role the mafia played in previous wildfires is revisited.

events.title: Secretary Hegseth grilled over growing cost of war with Iran
events.summary: Iran launched new attacks on U.S. allies in the region today even as Secretary Hegseth made his case to a Senate committee for more funding. The hearing turned contentious with a verbal dispute over leadership. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

events.title: Two American tourists hospitalized in stabbing attack near Greece’s Acropolis
events.summary: Greek police say that a man stabbed two American tourists in front of the entrance of the Acropolis in Athens, sending the couple to the hospital with injuries. NBC News’ Claudio Lavanga reports the man was arrested after police intervened.

events.title: President Trump vows revenge on Iran for killing U.S. service members
events.summary: After a weekend of deadly attacks, the Pentagon has released the identities of some of the fallen service members, including 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan from Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Texas, who were killed from an Iranian attack. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports from the region.

events.title: Trump approves landmark U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
events.summary: President Donald Trump has approved a landmark civilian nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia that is expected to be announced Wednesday, a senior administration official told NBC News.

events.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
events.summary: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

events.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
events.summary: The firing caps a crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

events.title: Andy Burnham becomes seventh British prime minister in a decade
events.summary: The new Labour Party leader, Andy Burnham, replaced Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.

events.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
events.summary: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

events.title: Graphic: The U.K. is in a new era of leadership turnover
events.summary: The U.K. has had seven prime ministers since 2016, compared with just three from 2000 to 2015.

events.title: Tate brothers arrested by U.S. authorities, face additional rape charges in the U.K.
events.summary: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by federal law enforcement officials in Miami on Saturday, the U.S.

events.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes after two U.S. service members killed in Jordan
events.summary: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

events.title: Iran and Kuwait report attacks on water and power infrastructure as strikes escalate
events.summary: Critical water and power infrastructure sites have been damaged as the U.S. and Iran continue to trade strikes, while the parched Middle East reels from temperatures of 100 degrees and beyond.

events.title: U.S. completes latest round of strikes on Iran’s military sites as Hormuz tensions climb
events.summary: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran late Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Pro-Kremlin blogger who turned on Putin is arrested as Russia steps up crackdown
events.summary: A former loyalist blogger turned fierce critic of Russian President Vladimir Putin has been detained and accused of spreading false information about the armed forces, state media reported Friday.
